user_scenarios.user_goal: As an ai_infra wiki maintainer, verify that known accelerator model sources are one-shot and new hardware discovery is monthly, candidate-based, and manually accepted.
prerequisites: The crawler workbench backend and frontend dependencies are available.
The accelerator monthly discovery implementation has been completed.
The source example YAML includes known accelerator model sources and monthly discovery sources.
user_scenarios.entrypoint: bash -lc 'REPO_ROOT=$(pwd) && cd personal-wiki/apps/crawler_workbench/backend && PYTHONPATH=. pytest -q tests/test_db_profiles.py tests/test_scheduler.py tests/test_discovery.py && cd ../frontend && npm test && npm run build && cd "$REPO_ROOT" && python personal-wiki/tools/wiki_cli/cli.py --root personal-wiki validate-accelerators && python personal-wiki/tools/wiki_cli/cli.py --root personal-wiki validate --domain ai_infra'
steps: Run backend profile, scheduler, and discovery tests.
Run frontend tests for source subscriptions and accelerator candidates.
Validate accelerator catalog metadata and the ai_infra wiki domain.
expected_outcomes: Known compute-accelerators model/spec profiles use run_policy once.
Scheduler skips one-shot sources after a successful evidence capture.
Monthly discovery profiles create or update accelerator candidates instead of creating formal source profiles automatically.
Candidate accept creates a one-shot source; candidate reject does not create a source.
The Sources page exposes run policy and pending accelerator candidates.
Wiki accelerator validation and ai_infra validation pass.
failure_signals: A known model source remains weekly or monthly scheduled without run_policy once.
A discovery source auto-creates formal source profiles without review.
Candidate deduplication creates repeated rows for the same vendor/model/evidence URL.
The frontend hides candidate evidence or cannot accept/reject candidates.
cleanup: Remove .codex/evaluations/tasks/compute-accelerator-monthly-discovery-01/ if a clean rerun is needed.
